payload.message: Link KV Cache Decode Latency with Long-Context RAG Tradeoffs

Link these notes because they capture two different performance constraints in LLM systems. KV Cache Decode Latency is about reducing decode-time recomputation and managing memory residency, while Long-Context RAG Tradeoffs is about retrieval precision, evidence coverage, and prompt budget efficiency. Engineers reasoning about end-to-end latency and answer quality should be able to move between these notes in both directions because the tradeoffs often interact in production systems.
